evidence: We continue to invest in global market trends around automation and digitalization, as well as technologies related to artificial intelligence (AI).
Over the last three years, we have invested $574 million in research and development ($199 million in 2025, $189 million in 2024, and $185 million in 2023)
We devote a substantial proportion of our research and development budget to software development.
AI-driven image recognition solutions for fresh goods
We have several initiatives that further strengthen our capabilities to serve customers, and we continue to advance the digital capability of our products and software
As we introduce AI and machine learning into our technology platform (as well as those of our customers through provision of our services)
The rapid evolution and increased adoption of artificial intelligence technologies amplify these [cybersecurity] concerns.
challenges with properly managing the use of AI could result in reputational harm, competitive harm, and legal liability, and adversely affect our results of operations
if we fail to adopt these new technologies, we may face price pressure from competitors using lower-cost AI systems

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: The $574M R&D figure is total R&D spend over three years, not AI-specific; no AI-dedicated budget or capex amount is disclosed, so score reflects named AI initiatives without quantified AI-specific spend.
ai_narrative_concreteness: AI image recognition for retail fresh goods is the only named deployed use case; other AI initiatives lack deployment status or quantified results, keeping score at 3.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section excerpts are present and contain company-specific AI risk scenarios with described mechanisms (regulatory compliance burden, competitive disadvantage, reputational/legal harm, cybersecurity amplification), but no quantification or named governance body, capping at 4.
